content.full_text:
January 15, 1990

Ms. Kathryn Donovan  
General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804  

Re: Request for Written Advice  

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I am seeking written advice regarding the use of "surplus" funds held by a local ballot measure committee. Specifically, does Government Code section 85807 apply to local measure committees and thus require a pro rata distribution of "surplus" contributions to contributors as opposed to distribution by some other method? Does it matter if the measure committee is "controlled" by a candidate?

It is not uncommon for local measure committees to have a surplus of funds at the end of a campaign. In some cases the committee may desire to use the funds for a future campaign or contribute the surplus to another committee supporting a similar issue. In other cases the committee would like to return the surplus to one or more contributors who were the major funding source of the committee.

Section 85807 states in part:

"Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat of a candidate for elective office, whichever occurs last, campaign funds raised after January 1, 1989, under the control of the former candidate or elected officer shall be considered surplus campaign funds..." (emphasis added).

This section is essentially the same as former Elections Code section 12404 and new section 12404, which applies only to funds raised before January 1, 1989. All three versions expressly refer to campaign funds raised by candidates for elective office.

Ms. Kathryn Donovan  
January 15, 1990  
Page 2

Interestingly, when the Legislature transferred the "personal use" laws to the jurisdiction of the Commission it revised former Elections Code section 12401 (now Gov't Code § 58800) to now specifically include "ballot measure committees" under its provisions. The Legislature did not make a similar revision to Government Code section 85807 even though both revisions were made in the same legislation (SB 1431). This decision not to make a similar revision to the "surplus funds" provision evidences some intent by the Legislature not to subject ballot measure committees to that provision.

Please provide guidance in this regard.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
THOMAS W. HILTACHK

TWH/kab
